text: Managing a team requires a mix of skills to keep everyone working well together. First, clear communication is essential so that tasks and goals are understood by all team members. Once objectives are set, effective delegation involves assigning tasks based on each person's strengths and workload. Providing regular feedback is also important, as it helps individuals improve and feel valued. Additionally, a good leader knows how to maintain motivation by recognizing achievements and encouraging progress. When disagreements arise, conflict resolution skills are needed, such as listening to both sides and finding a fair compromise. By focusing on these areas, you can build a productive and harmonious team.
